last_analyst_note:
Mode 2b run @ 2026-04-26T09:06:57.427Z.

DISPATCHER TRACE (against fresh clean clone, refreshed at run start from origin/main):
  Mode 0 — WIN onboarding: workspace=71, GitHub=71. NO WORK.
  Mode 1 — Expansion queue: 0 actionable pending (next_id was 258, now 259 after this run). NO WORK.
  Mode 1b — Prediction writeups: ALL DONE (96 entries, all have our_verdict). NO WORK.
  Mode 2 — Human notes: NO NOTES (HNOTE-007 still pending decider integration of EXP-255/256/257 — analyst no-op per prior runs' guidance).
  Mode 2b — Attention inbox: 70 pending. *** ANOMALY *** the prior three analyst runs (07:04Z, ~05:14Z) reported "empty / NO WORK" but the dispatcher check returns 70 pending items dating back to 2026-04-12. The exact dispatcher snippet from analyst.md returns "ATTENTION ITEMS: 70 pending" against both FUSE and the fresh clone — items are real. Possible explanations: (a) prior runs ran a slightly different version of the snippet that mis-handled the array shape; (b) the inbox was emptied and re-populated by the decider between runs; (c) prior runs simply mis-reported. This run elected to actually process one item per the prompt's instruction.
  Mode 3 — Defenses: 0 surviving. NO WORK.
  Mode 4 — Globe fingerprint: ALL DONE.
  Assigned-analyst issues: 0. NO WORK.
  Normal mode: changes_pending_analysis=0. NO WORK.

ATTENTION INBOX WORK PERFORMED (one item per Mode 2b protocol):
  Item: ATT-2026-04-25T05:27:36Z (target_type=win, target_id=WIN-010, pushed_by=decider, pushed_at=2026-04-25T05:27:36Z).
  Reason: decider processed WIN-010.c10 review and created ISS-1522 (Chapman-Sq hyphenation), ISS-1523 (multiplicative-error claim needs proof or softening, persisting c6-c9 hole), ISS-1524 (K&C page range). Decider asked analyst to check ISS-1523 especially.
  Investigation: read WIN-010.c10 curmudgeon review and the WIN-010 detail_verdict_text in data/wins.json. Confirmed the target sentence ("any nonlinear-coverage effect will introduce an approximately common multiplicative error on both residuals at first order, so the geographic-vs-magnetic-latitude fingerprint remains the dominant discriminator even under moderate nonlinear-coverage corrections") occurs exactly once in wins.json, in WIN-010 detail_verdict_text only.
  Kernel of truth surfaced: the residual numerator R_i = O_i / (baseline × coverage_i) is literally the same number in both fits — only the comparison target (FSF_i geographic vs f(mag_lat_i) magnetic) differs. So a multiplicative coverage correction m(c_i) = g(c_i)/c_i contaminates BOTH fits identically as a per-station scale factor. "Approximately common" is plausibly correct at α near 1 in g(c) = c^α (∼20% station-to-station spread for European coverage range 0.4-0.95 with α ∈ [0.8, 1.2], comfortably below the 50%+ profile differences between FSF and f(mag_lat) at LER and HAD). What the WIN does NOT do is demonstrate this — it just asserts it. Curmudgeon recommends either a worked example (option a) or softening + pre-registration (option b).
  Output: EXP-258, single substring replace_substring patch on WIN-010 detail_verdict_text. Replaces unargued first-order claim with sub-dominance expectation + pre-registered post-2026-08-12 sensitivity check at α ∈ {0.8, 1.0, 1.2} that produces a falsifiable binary outcome (ranking stable / ranking flips). Cleaner than curmudgeon's literal phrasing (which had a "in 2-3 the post-event analysis" typo).
  Inbox marked resolved with EXP-258 and proposal cross-references.

USER-PREFERENCES DIRECTIVE ('Think about the other side of the argument too') — counter-arguments considered before electing patch:
  (a) Should I have done option (a) — worked-example demonstration — instead of option (b)? CONSIDERED: option (a) requires station-by-station coverage values for 2026-08-12 plus a hand-computation of m_i spread under α ∈ [0.8, 1.2], which is doable but turns the WIN body into a citation-and-arithmetic essay. Option (b) is shorter, more honest about what is and isn't derived in the WIN, and produces a falsifiable post-event report rather than an asserted bound. The kernel-of-truth section in the EXP records the option (a) argument so a future decider can elevate it if a worked example becomes available.
  (b) Should I have just deleted the sentence entirely? REJECTED: silence on a five-cycle-flagged hole is worse than an honest pre-registered check. Defenders would point out the WIN ignored a known systematic.
  (c) Should I have processed ALL 70 inbox items in one run? REJECTED: prompt says "Process one item per run." Backlog is flagged in the dispatcher trace above; if it represents a chronic problem, that is a tinker / human escalation, not an analyst greedy-process.
  (d) Should I have escalated the inbox-vs-prior-runs discrepancy to tinker? PARTIAL: documented it in this note (the dispatcher trace explicitly flags the ANOMALY) but did not write a tinker-targeted item, since the protocol is for the analyst to surface inbox state in its own run note. Tinker reads status.json daily; the discrepancy will be visible.

WORKSPACE / CLONE PARITY: clone (refreshed) and FUSE both report wins.json offset 62604 for the before-string. expansion-tracker next_id was 258 in both, now 259 in FUSE only (decider will pull on next run; workspace-sync rescues if needed since expansion-tracker is unclassified multi-writer per CLAUDE.md).

OUTPUTS WRITTEN THIS RUN (FUSE):
  monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-258-WIN010-nonlinear-coverage-soften-iss1523.json (NEW)
  monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1523-resolution.json (NEW)
  monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json (next_id 258→259, items 256→257)
  monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json (ATT-2026-04-25T05:27:36Z marked resolved; pending 70→69)
  monitor/status.json (this update)

CANARY STATUS: opentimestamps-provenance trap remains ACTIVE. Did not check chg files this run (Mode 2b consumed the slot); poller traces in prior status note remain current.

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE: 69 pending attention-inbox items remain. If subsequent analyst runs continue to mis-report this as empty, escalate to tinker — the prompt's dispatcher snippet returned 70 pending today, so a buggy reading would represent regression. Decider will see EXP-258 on its next run via clone refresh; ISS-1523 should close once the patch applies. ISS-1522 (Chapman-Sq hyphenation cluster) and ISS-1524 (K&C TOC verification footnote — note: K&C 2040-2049 page range itself was already corrected by EXP-244, so ISS-1524 may be either nominal or asking for the additional TOC link footnote) remain open for separate handling.
